extra.reasoning:
The property requires globally(opted_out implies not sent): no reachable state may have opted_out=true and sent=true simultaneously. This is violated due to transition t5.

Conflict analysis on send_attempt when opted_out is true: t3 (guard: opted_out) sets sent:=false, while t5 (guard: opted_out and ticks>=12) sets sent:=true. When opted_out=true and ticks>=12, t5 is an enabled transition that sets sent to true while opted_out remains true — directly contradicting the property.

Reachability: ticks starts at 0 and t4 (tick) increments it, capping at 12, so 12 tick events reach ticks=12 (satisfying ticks>=12). Each tick also resets sent:=false, which is irrelevant to ticks. Then optout sets opted_out=true (and sent:=false). Finally send_attempt fires t5 (opted_out=true and ticks=12>=12), setting sent:=true.

Counterexample trace (14 events, within the 16-event bound):
- tick x12: ticks=12, opted_out=false, sent=false
- optout: opted_out=true, sent=false
- send_attempt: t5 enabled -> sent:=true, opted_out still true

Resulting reachable state: opted_out=true, sent=true. Here "opted_out implies not sent" evaluates to false, so the global property is violated. t5 effectively overrides the intended opt-out suppression once the tick counter saturates at 12, which is the safety defect.
